  </svg>
  <text
    x="40"
    y="21"
    font-family="system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif"
    font-size="15"
    font-weight="600"
    fill="#2f5d50"
  >Memories</text>
</svg>

# Memories — brand assets

**Purpose:** Canonical vectors and designer-facing notes for **Memories** (photos, voice, transcripts; elder-friendly capture). This folder is **not** the web root; the Vite app serves icons from [`apps/web/public/`](../apps/web/public/).

**Master symbol:** [`symbol-master.svg`](./symbol-master.svg) — polaroid frame + waveform (still reads at **16×16**). **Full lockup:** [`logo-full-color.svg`](./logo-full-color.svg).

**Workflow:** Edit masters here first, then copy or re-export into `apps/web/public/` (favicon, PWA, in-app chrome). Regenerate raster favicons when you add an ICO/PNG pipeline.

---

## Voice and positioning

- **What we are:** A calm, trustworthy place to **preserve moments** before letting go of objects — pictures, voice, and text working together.
- **Tone:** Plain language, warm neutral, never playful at the expense of clarity (**NFR-012**). Prefer reassurance over hype (“Saved,” “We’ll finish when you’re online”).
- **Visual bias:** Soft contrast, generous spacing, large tap targets; avoid loud gradients or fashion-palette churn on core flows.

---

## Color tokens (initial)

Use these as the default UI pairing until design ships a full palette in wireframes or hi-fi.

| Token | Hex | Role |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Brand / primary surface** | `#2f5d50` | Symbol, key actions, focused chrome |
| **Brand wash / canvas** | `#eef4f1` | App background tints, symbol backdrop |
| **Neutral text** | `#444`–`#666` | Body and secondary copy on light surfaces |

Dark mode: derive by lowering saturation of `#2f5d50` and deepening the wash; keep WCAG contrast on body text per [design-wireframe-v1.md](../docs/design-wireframe-v1.md) and TDD accessibility notes.

---

## Deliverables checklist

| Deliverable | Role | Master file |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Symbol** | Favicon, PWA, compact UI | `symbol-master.svg` |
| **Full logo** | Marketing, sign-in hero | `logo-full-color.svg` |
| **Monochrome** | Small sizes, print, email | *Add `logo-mono-dark.svg` / `logo-mono-light.svg` when needed* |
| **Favicon set** | Tab + touch icons | Optional `favicon-source.png`; sync [`apps/web/public/favicon.svg`](../apps/web/public/favicon.svg) with symbol |

**Constraints:** Geometry stays simple; detailed UI color rules stay aligned with [`docs/design-wireframe-v1.md`](../docs/design-wireframe-v1.md).

---

## After you change the symbol

1. Update every bundled duplicate (`apps/web/public/favicon.svg`, any inline marks, PWA sources).
2. Regenerate ICO/PNG and manifest icons if your pipeline uses rasters.
3. Confirm `apps/web/index.html` `<link rel="icon" …>` paths still resolve.

---

## Revision

| Date | Notes |
| --- | --- |
| 2026-04-30 | Imported `brand/` pattern from Ohana `cursor-template`; Memories symbol, lockup, voice/color notes, favicon sync |

</svg>

- **Touched:** `brand/`, `apps/web/public/favicon.svg`, `apps/web/src/App.tsx`, `scripts/verify-template.ps1`, `scripts/verify-template.sh`, `README.md`, `NEW_PROJECT_CHECKLIST.md`, `TEMPLATE_SYNC.md`, `docs/implementation-log.md`

content.text:
Here’s what was done.

### Source

Branding was pulled from your sibling repo  
`C:\Users\Ken Levy\OneDrive\Documents\Business\Ohana\cursor-template`  
(`brand/README.md`, `brand/symbol-master.svg`, and the same `verify-template.*` requirements).

### Added in Memories

| Path | Purpose |
|------|--------|
| [`brand/README.md`](brand/README.md) | Product branding notes: positioning, tone (aligned with **NFR-012**), initial color tokens, checklist, sync steps |
| [`brand/symbol-master.svg`](brand/symbol-master.svg) | Master mark: polaroid + small waveform (photo + voice), readable at 16×16 |
| [`brand/logo-full-color.svg`](brand/logo-full-color.svg) | Horizontal lockup: symbol + “Memories” (system UI fonts) |
| [`apps/web/public/favicon.svg`](apps/web/public/favicon.svg) | Same geometry as the master symbol (comment says to keep in sync) |

`index.html` already pointed at `/favicon.svg`; that file was missing, so the browser tab icon should work now.

### Wired through the repo

- [`scripts/verify-template.ps1`](scripts/verify-template.ps1) and [`scripts/verify-template.sh`](scripts/verify-template.sh) now require `brand/README.md` and `brand/symbol-master.svg` (matching the template).
- [`README.md`](README.md) layout table includes `brand/`.
- [`NEW_PROJECT_CHECKLIST.md`](NEW_PROJECT_CHECKLIST.md) has a branding sync bullet.
- [`TEMPLATE_SYNC.md`](TEMPLATE_SYNC.md) lists `brand/` for future copies.
- [`apps/web/src/App.tsx`](apps/web/src/App.tsx) shows the mark on the splash and uses `#2f5d50` for the title.
- [`docs/implementation-log.md`](docs/implementation-log.md) has a short entry for this work.

### Brand choices (for iteration)

- **Colors:** `#2f5d50` (primary), `#eef4f1` (wash), neutrals `#444`–`#666` for body — calm, healthcare-adjacent, not noisy.
- **Symbol:** Simple geometry so it stays clear for elders and small favicons; easy to swap if design gives you a final mark.

**Checks:** `.\scripts\verify-template.ps1` completed (expected warnings for `cursor-template` / `@your-org` mentions), and `npm run typecheck --workspace=@memories/web` passed.

If you want to go further, the next concrete steps are: add mono SVGs for email/print, a raster favicon pipeline (ICO/PNG + manifest), and aligning CSS variables in `App.css` with the tokens in `brand/README.md`. I can do that when you’re ready.

content.text:
Simplifying the icon: corner path strokes may render inconsistently. Keeping the frame and heart for a clearer remembrance motif.

[REDACTED]

Here’s what changed.

### [`brand/logo-full-color.svg`](brand/logo-full-color.svg)

- **Wordmark:** **Legacy** (same typography as before, slightly tighter `letter-spacing`).
- **Mark:** A **soft picture frame** (`#faf8f5` fill, `#2f5d50` stroke) on the usual wash background — reads like a **treasured photo or keepsake** on display.
- **Center:** A **warm rose heart** (`#b86b5c`) for **love and what matters most** in someone’s life — i.e. remembering someone or something central to an elder’s story.

The app symbol (`symbol-master.svg` / favicon) is unchanged so the **Memories** product mark stays separate from this **Legacy** marketing lockup.

### [`brand/README.md`](brand/README.md)

- Describes the Legacy lockup (frame + heart).
- Adds token **`#b86b5c`** — Legacy accent / remembrance warmth.

If you want **Legacy** on the live splash or favicon too, say how you’d like product name vs. campaign name to read (e.g. “Memories — Legacy” vs. replacing the title entirely).

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s a simpler approach that reads more clearly as a **picture frame**:

### What changed

The lockup icon is now **three stacked rounded rectangles** (no heart, no extra strokes):

1. **Outer ring (`#2f5d50`)** — reads as the **frame molding** (visible band on all sides).  
2. **Middle (`#f4efe6`)** — **mat** / inner border.  
3. **Inner (`#d4e4dc`)** — **opening** where the photo would sit (soft fill so it isn’t empty white).

Same brand wash behind it (`#eef4f1`), **Legacy** wordmark unchanged.

### Why it stays simple

- One visual idea: **nested frame → mat → picture**.  
- No icons inside the opening, so nothing competes with “this is a frame.”  
- Still works small because the bands stay thick enough at 32×32.

[`brand/README.md`](brand/README.md) is updated so the lockup is described as the nested frame and the old heart accent token is replaced with mat / opening colors.

If you want it even more “gallery frame,” the next step would be a **polaroid** variant (heavier bottom bar only)—same complexity, different silhouette. Say if you want that instead.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
I like it except add the heart back in the middle and keep the thick green and move the light green to be a mat inside the dark green
</user_query>

Swapping layers: light sage mat inside the dark green frame, cream inner opening for the heart, restoring the warm heart.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what you have now:

### [`brand/logo-full-color.svg`](brand/logo-full-color.svg)

1. **Dark green** `#2f5d50` — same thick outer frame (5→8 inset, 3px molding on each side).  
2. **Light green** `#d4e4dc` — mat **inside** that frame (the band between the dark green and the center).  
3. **Cream** `#faf8f5` — inner “photo” area so the heart doesn’t sit on green.  
4. **Heart** `#b86b5c` — centered in the opening, scaled to the 12×12 inner rect.

So the sage tone is the **mat**, not the center field. [`brand/README.md`](brand/README.md) is updated to match (mat, opening, heart tokens + lockup description).

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
Nice, can you make the heart bigger and centered
</user_query>

Replacing the heart with a larger symmetric path sized to the inner opening (≈10.65–21.35 × 10.75–21.25), centered in the cream square.

[REDACTED]

Here’s what changed:

### Heart size & placement

The old heart was a small cubic path sitting low in the square. It’s now a **single symmetric heart** drawn with cubics so it:

- Spans almost the full **cream opening** (~10.65–21.35 horizontally, ~10.75–21.25 vertically), with a little air for the **1px corner radius**.
- Is **centered on x = 16** with matching left/right lobes (`14.15` / `17.85` spacing).
- Has the **cusp at (16, 11.65)** and the **tip at (16, 21.25)** so it sits visually centered in the 12×12 inner field.

So it reads larger, balanced, and centered without overlapping the mat band. If you want it to bleed even closer to the edges, we can nudge the coordinates another ~0.25–0.5px.

[REDACTED]

Great. Now please use this for the symbol and faveicon, and any other items. We are only using Legacy and not Memories.
</user_query>

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 32 32" role="img" aria-label="Legacy product symbol">

input.contents:
# Legacy — brand assets

**Purpose:** Canonical vectors and designer-facing notes for **Legacy** — capture that honors what matters in an elder’s life (photos, voice, transcripts; elder-friendly flows). This folder is **not** the web root; the Vite app serves icons from [`apps/web/public/`](../apps/web/public/).

**Master symbol:** [`symbol-master.svg`](./symbol-master.svg) — picture frame (dark molding, sage mat, cream field, heart); tuned for **16×16**. **Full lockup:** [`logo-full-color.svg`](./logo-full-color.svg) — wordmark **Legacy** plus the same icon.

**Workflow:** Edit masters here first, then copy or re-export into `apps/web/public/` (favicon, PWA, in-app chrome). Regenerate raster favicons when you add an ICO/PNG pipeline.

---

## Voice and positioning

- **What we are:** A calm, trustworthy place to **preserve moments** before letting go of objects — pictures, voice, and text working together.
- **Tone:** Plain language, warm neutral, never playful at the expense of clarity (**NFR-012**). Prefer reassurance over hype (“Saved,” “We’ll finish when you’re online”).
- **Visual bias:** Soft contrast, generous spacing, large tap targets; avoid loud gradients or fashion-palette churn on core flows.

---

## Color tokens (initial)

Use these as the default UI pairing until design ships a full palette in wireframes or hi-fi.

| Token | Hex | Role |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Brand / primary surface** | `#2f5d50` | Symbol, key actions, focused chrome |
| **Brand wash / canvas** | `#eef4f1` | App background tints, symbol backdrop |
| **Mat (sage)** | `#d4e4dc` | Lockup mat inside dark green frame |
| **Opening / paper** | `#faf8f5` | Lockup inner field behind heart |
| **Legacy accent (warm)** | `#b86b5c` | Lockup heart |
| **Neutral text** | `#444`–`#666` | Body and secondary copy on light surfaces |

Dark mode: derive by lowering saturation of `#2f5d50` and deepening the wash; keep WCAG contrast on body text per [design-wireframe-v1.md](../docs/design-wireframe-v1.md) and TDD accessibility notes.

---

## Deliverables checklist

| Deliverable | Role | Master file |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Symbol** | Favicon, PWA, compact UI | `symbol-master.svg` |
| **Full logo** | Marketing, sign-in hero (“Legacy” + frame mark) | `logo-full-color.svg` |
| **Monochrome** | Small sizes, print, email | *Add `logo-mono-dark.svg` / `logo-mono-light.svg` when needed* |
| **Favicon set** | Tab + touch icons | Optional `favicon-source.png`; sync [`apps/web/public/favicon.svg`](../apps/web/public/favicon.svg) with symbol |

**Constraints:** Geometry stays simple; detailed UI color rules stay aligned with [`docs/design-wireframe-v1.md`](../docs/design-wireframe-v1.md).

---

## After you change the symbol

1. Update every bundled duplicate (`apps/web/public/favicon.svg`, any inline marks, PWA sources).
2. Regenerate ICO/PNG and manifest icons if your pipeline uses rasters.
3. Confirm `apps/web/index.html` `<link rel="icon" …>` paths still resolve.

---

## Revision

| Date | Notes |
| --- | --- |
| 2026-04-30 | Imported `brand/` pattern from Ohana `cursor-template`; Legacy symbol, lockup, voice/color notes, favicon sync |
| 2026-04-30 | Unified symbol + favicon with Legacy frame + heart; product brand Legacy |

# Legacy

Product (**Legacy**): capture and preserve what matters with **photos**, **recorded voice**, and **transcription** (to be implemented). The **Dashboard** and other platform apps live in separate repositories; this repo ships the Legacy vertical (**npm** workspaces `@memories/*`, Node + TypeScript API, React + TypeScript web, shared contracts).

When your layout or quality gates differ from the defaults below, update this file and the matching sections of `docs/` and CI so agents and humans see one story.

## Entries

### 2026-04-30 - Legacy product branding (symbol, UI copy, health id)

- **Area:** Web, Shared, API, Docs
- **Summary:** Unified **`brand/symbol-master.svg`** and **`apps/web/public/favicon.svg`** with the Legacy frame + heart lockup; **`serviceName`** → `legacy`; **`GET /health`** / **`HEAD /health`** body **`service`** → `legacy-api`; startup logs say Legacy API. **`apps/web`** title and splash use **Legacy**; **`README`**, **`AGENTS.md`**, **`brand/README.md`**, root **`package.json`** description updated for customer-facing name. **JWT `aud`** stays **`memories-api`** (`.env.example`, local-auth helper, tests) so existing IdP tokens keep working; REST paths and DB identifiers unchanged.
- **Touched:** `brand/symbol-master.svg`, `brand/logo-full-color.svg`, `brand/README.md`, `apps/web/public/favicon.svg`, `apps/web/index.html`, `apps/web/src/App.tsx`, `packages/shared/src/index.ts`, `packages/shared/src/index.test.ts`, `apps/api/src/app.ts`, `apps/api/src/app.test.ts`, `apps/api/src/index.ts`, `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `package.json`, `docs/implementation-log.md`
- **Validation:** `npm run test`

### 2026-04-30 - Brand folder + Memories symbol

content.text:
Here’s what was applied.

### Visual (same mark everywhere)

- **`brand/symbol-master.svg`** — Legacy frame + heart (matches lockup).
- **`apps/web/public/favicon.svg`** — Same geometry; comments point at `symbol-master.svg`.
- **`brand/logo-full-color.svg`** — Icon block unchanged vs symbol (comment notes sync).

### Product-facing copy

- **`apps/web/index.html`** — `<title>Legacy</title>`
- **`apps/web/src/App.tsx`** — `Legacy` heading, `legacy-splash`, logo `alt="Legacy"`, tagline uses “Preserve…”, `serviceName` still shown as **`legacy`**.

### Shared + API identifiers

- **`packages/shared`** — `serviceName = "legacy"` (test updated).
- **`apps/api`** — `GET`/`HEAD` `/health` now returns **`"service":"legacy-api"`** (test updated); **`apps/api/src/index.ts`** logs **Legacy API**.
- **Unchanged on purpose:** **`JWT_AUDIENCE=memories-api`** in `.env.example`, **`local-auth-dev.ts`**, and **`app.test.ts`** issuer/audience — so issued tokens and IdP config keep working. Routes remain **`/memories`**, packages **`@memories/*`**, DB **`memories`** — domain/API naming, not marketing.

### Docs / meta

- **`README.md`**, **`AGENTS.md`**, **`brand/README.md`** — describe **Legacy** as the product; note **`@memories/*`** as workspace names where relevant.
- **`package.json`** — description updated for Legacy.
- **`docs/implementation-log.md`** — short entry for this rebrand.

### Still saying “Memories” today

Editioned specs (**PRD**, **TDD**, **development plan**, ADRs, etc.) were **not** bulk-renamed; that’s a larger editorial pass if you want titles and requirement text aligned to Legacy everywhere.

**Tests:** `npm run test` passed (`@memories/api`, `@memories/shared`).

[REDACTED]
